updates.tweet_text: Closing update #ES_F: Volatility still totally gone and we are at the exact same level we were 24hrs ago. 7506 Failed Breakdown was todays long 9am. Targets were 7522, 7532, 7548 (hit). Just 7575 left. Newsletter was sent early with entries for the next selloff if we get one
updates.notes: All referenced levels (7506, 7522, 7532, 7548, 7575) are either morning-plan levels or observation/hindsight references to targets already hit. No new actionable levels named.

updates.tweet_text:
We remain in low volatility, rangebound summer trading in #ES_F for 3 weeks now. Yesterday's long was the 7506 trap. Targets were 7522, 7532, 7548, 7575. 3/4 hit.

Plan today: 7506 must reclaim with volatility to see 7516, 7522, 7533, 7548+. Watch 72 traps below now https://t.co/xqbs5pUL5f
updates.notes: All referenced levels (7506, 7533, 7548, 7575) are morning-plan reaffirmations; 7516/7522 are intermediate targets, no new actionable levels named.

updates.tweet_text:
We are finally getting a little volatility and range expansion in #ES_F which gives day traders something to work with (these bursts are rare until September).

7472 shelf must reclaim with heavy vol to return to 7492, 7506+. 7459, 7440 are below. 7472-7627 remains macro range https://t.co/E2His3in0i
updates.notes: 7472 is close to morning plan 7473; 7492 not named with actionable language; 7459/7440/7506 all in morning plan. No genuinely new major levels.

updates.tweet_text: @SuzieQ81k Not quite a knife here, more a slow drip down (summer trading slow even on the red phases), but still no buying deep red though! My hardest rule

updates.tweet_text:
We are working down levels nicely in #ES_F and excellent to see some range expansion after over 3 weeks in the same range. These expansions are rare in summer, so have to capitalize quickly. 7459 was next down, here now.

7440, 7422 below. Full control to bears until 72 recovers https://t.co/efzBASVggR
updates.notes: All prices mentioned (7459, 7440, 7422) are morning-plan levels. '72 recovers' references 7472-area, also in plan. Pure reaffirmation/observation.

updates.tweet_text: @investingpro12 My system doesn't buy red. Nothing wrong with it, many traders do but I never 1) Short and 2) Never buy red especially after a structural breakdown. If a range break like this sticks we could easily sell hundreds of points
updates.notes: No price levels mentioned at all; purely a philosophical/methodological commentary about not buying red or shorting after structural breakdown.

updates.tweet_text: @MaxSkowron Monday and Friday. Its been the range all month though which has now broken down so I wouldn't touch any longs now (as I mentioned in newsletter) until or unless 7472 recovers. Major structural breakdown could sell for a long time from here if we don't get a catalyst
updates.notes: 7472 is close to morning plan 7473 (rounding/same level); conversational reply with no new explicitly actionable price meeting all three criteria.

updates.tweet_text: @StephenJ223624 It was an immediate backtest of 7575 but typically price also backtest it after the breakdown selloff occurs, from lower. 7575 remains a magnet that has to be backtested
updates.notes: 7575 is already in the morning plan; tweet is a reaffirmation/commentary on its magnetic property, not a new actionable level.

updates.tweet_text:
We are settling into chop again now in #ES_F and today is all about 7472 which is the bull/bear line. As posted 1hr ago, full control to bears until 72 recoveries. We just took 1 crack at it, went +10, then back down again

No change: 72 reclaims see 7483, 7491, 7506+. Bottoms it https://t.co/oQgkJNOSV6
updates.notes: 7472 is near morning plan 7473; 7483/7491/7506 are reaffirmations or observations of prior levels. No new actionable levels introduced.

updates.tweet_text: @RobyArnoldi It would go below the lowest low if its a 10% runner. If it only gets to the 1st profit take at 83 though which it did on the first run then you need to exit slightly below break-even to preserve a green trade
updates.notes: Reply tweet with trade management advice (exit near break-even, 1st profit take at ~7483); no new actionable level named, just managing an existing position.

updates.tweet_text: @RobyArnoldi Yes 15 points full size is the max loss I'd ever take. If a larger stop is required though, you can half the trade size. A half size trade for example a 30 point stop would equal 15 on a full trade.
updates.notes: Reply explaining risk/position sizing rules. No price levels named, only stop-loss point distances (15, 30) used as math examples, not ES prices.

updates.tweet_text: @thebraithwaits Yes that's how they should look. Speed and momentum

updates.tweet_text:

At 7440 support. 7422 below https://t.co/tHMm9eOs8o
